description: Backlog of features. The leader dispatches one pending feature at a time whose depends_on are all done; the implementer builds it; the reviewer approves before it flips to done.

model_provider.name: OpenRouter
model_provider.notes: OpenAI-compatible gateway; one API key proxies both DeepSeek tiers. All model access goes through it — no direct DeepSeek or Anthropic SDK.

sdk.name: OpenAI Agents SDK
sdk.notes: Orchestration primitive (agent + tool loop), pointed at OpenRouter's base_url. The deterministic controller still owns ALL routing; the SDK's tool_input_guardrail supplies the code-side pre-tool-call deny gate (nested-agent + .env blocks).

researcher.why: Only heavy multi-turn tool user (web + file); cheap-tier tool-call accuracy collapses on multi-turn orchestration (BFCL V4), and a fabricated or mis-formatted citation is a hard-to-detect, hard-to-reverse leak into the thesis.

writer.why: Produces the actual deliverable and is the only agent that repairs its own work across many conflicting-feedback revision turns; gates detect but never fix, and at low document volume the Flash saving is trivial against chapter quality.

reviewer.why: Read-only, non-terminal, 2-pass soft gate backstopped by the Pro Judge; worst case is one wasted Writer revision — the single bounded/backstopped place to spend the cheap tier.

judge.why: Terminal pass/fail gate with no downstream check (a false APPROVE ships); mirrors the original's deliberate opus-for-the-gate choice. Quote machine-verification guards the evidence, not the judgment, so the reasoning itself must be the strongest tier.

notes: Per-agent tiers were decided via a 4-lens judge panel; the agentic-tool-reliability and faithful-port lenses both landed on pro/pro/flash/pro.

Known limitation: Judge and Writer share one model family + tier, so the verifier partly shares the generator's blind spots (verification drifts toward self-agreement). Mitigated by the machine-verified verbatim-quote requirement, the fixed rubric, and the two human checkpoints.

Reviewer is the closest call and the pipeline's thin spot — escalate flash -> pro if it starts rubber-stamping (Judge rejects drafts the Reviewer approved in >~30% of runs, thin/contradictory feedback, or {approved, feedback} parse failures).

Structured output for Reviewer/Judge uses prompting + code-side validation, NOT native tool-call JSON (DeepSeek native function-calling accuracy collapses ~50pts vs prompting on BFCL V4). Keep the controller's verbatim-quote machine-check.
Model slugs are config-driven; the deepseek-v4-* names supersede the retired deepseek-chat / deepseek-reasoner aliases — re-verify the current slugs on OpenRouter at implementation time.
At most one feature may be 'in_progress' at a time.
A feature may only be 'in_progress' or 'done' if all its depends_on are 'done'.
Only the implementer flips a feature to 'done', and only after reviewer approval.
Each feature lists the files it may touch in 'paths' and its 'acceptance' criteria.

features.name: project_skeleton
acceptance: uv-managed package skeleton per .claude/rules/architecture.md §3 (config.py + package layers + tests).
config.py is the only module reading os.environ; exposes load_config().
uv run pytest tests -q -m 'not integration' is green with at least one smoke test.
uv run ruff check . and ruff format --check . are clean.
bash init.sh finishes with [OK] Environment ready.

features.name: openrouter_client
acceptance: The OpenAI Agents SDK is configured against OpenRouter's OpenAI-compatible base_url (stack.model_provider.base_url); the API key is read only in config.py from env, with no default, and fails loudly if missing.
The two model slugs (stack.models.pro / stack.models.flash) are config constants, never inlined at call sites.
Per-call token usage is captured for cost accounting, and a per-stage maxTurns cap is enforceable.
Unit test mocks the HTTP/client layer (no real network call) and asserts the client targets the OpenRouter base_url and selects the requested tier.
uv run ruff check . clean; bash init.sh green.

features.name: agent_definitions
acceptance: The four agents (researcher, writer, reviewer, judge) are defined with the per-agent model tier from stack.agent_models (researcher=pro, writer=pro, reviewer=flash, judge=pro) and least-privilege tool scoping (only the researcher gets web tools).

A code-side pre-tool-call guardrail (OpenAI Agents SDK tool_input_guardrail, or an equivalent dispatcher wrapper) denies nested agent/task spawning and any tool access to .env — enforced in code, not by prompt.
Agent system prompts live in prompts/ as files, not inline strings.

Reviewer and Judge produce their structured output via prompting + code-side jsonschema validation (not native tool-call JSON); the Judge's per-criterion verbatim quotes remain machine-verifiable against the draft.
Unit tests assert each agent's model slug and tool scope, and that the guardrail denies a .env read and a nested-spawn attempt.
uv run ruff check . clean; bash init.sh green.
